description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages go, build, test, downstream, tag, and deploy and using the docker:stable-dind service. Use a .go template that runs on golang:1.13.6 and defines go build, go lint and go test jobs in the go stage; go build runs `go get ./...` then `go build -o ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/dependency-scanning` with CGO_ENABLED=0 and stores the dependency‑scanning directory as an artifact, go lint runs `go get -u golang.org/x/lint/golint` and `golint -set_exit_status`, and go test runs `go get ./...` and `go test -race -cover -v ./...`. Add a build commit job in the build stage using docker:stable that logs in to the registry, builds a temporary image with `docker build -t $TMP_IMAGE --build-arg DS_ANALYZER_IMAGE_TAG=$MAJOR .` and pushes it. Add an integration test job in the test stage that depends on go build, uses docker:stable and runs `./test.sh` with DS_ANALYZER_IMAGE_TAG set to $MAJOR. Define a .qa-downstream-ds template for downstream jobs that disables all security scans and runs in the downstream stage; create downstream QA jobs for java‑gradle, java‑maven, js‑npm, js‑yarn, php‑composer, python‑pip, python‑pipenv, ruby‑bundler, ruby‑bundler‑rails, ruby‑bundler‑js‑yarn, scala‑sbt, and go‑modules, each triggering the corresponding downstream project. Add a .docker_tag template that runs on docker:stable, logs in, pulls $TMP_IMAGE, tags it as $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:${IMAGE_TAG:-$CI_JOB_NAME} and pushes it. Create tag branch, tag edge and tag version jobs in the tag stage using this template with appropriate IMAGE_TAG values and rules (branches except master, master only, tags manual with changelog check). Define a .release template extending .docker_tag that runs only on tags, and create release jobs for each stable version (11‑10‑stable … 13‑0‑stable, latest, major) in the deploy stage that push the final image. Include SAST, Container‑Scanning and License‑Management templates and set variables DOCKER_DRIVER, DS_DEFAULT_ANALYZERS, MAJOR=2 and TMP_IMAGE.
